ocr_mistral.text:
![img-0.jpeg](img-0.jpeg)

# DIAGRAM OF THE FORWARD SECTION OF SPUTNIK II

1—Protective nose cone jettisoned after the satellite reached its orbit.
2—Instruments for studying the sun's radiation (ultra-violet and X-rays).
3—Sphere containing two radio transmitters and other instruments.
4—Frame enclosing the satellite's scientific equipment.
5—Airtight cabin for experimental dog.

PHOTOGRAPH OF THE FORWARD SECTION SHOWN IN DIAGRAM ABOVE. THE WEIGHT OF EQUIPMENT AND DOG WAS 1,130 POUNDS—SEX TIMES MORE THAN SPUTNIK I.

![img-1.jpeg](img-1.jpeg)
